parts.title: Modern Languages Teachers
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a [grade X] class in [language, e.g. Spanish] that meets the curriculum goals for [specific focus, e.g. oral interaction], with three activities and a warm-up.
Give me five strategies for motivating a [grade X] student who is afraid to speak [language] in the classroom, with one encouraging idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class the basics of [specific skill, e.g. verb conjugation in [language]], with three steps and one example.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. culture in [language]-speaking countries] into a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class, with one activity for each.
Create a list of five short encouraging phrases in [language] to build a [grade X] student's confidence during a language task.
Give me an example of a routine for starting a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class, with three steps and a simple conversation activity in the target language.
Suggest three ways to use everyday topics (e.g. [specific topic, e.g. food]) in a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class, with one activity for each.
Write a short template for a 10-minute lesson closing in [language] for a [grade X] class, with three components (e.g. reflection, performance).
Give me five ideas for making a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class more playful, focusing on [specific goal, e.g. vocabulary], with one game for each.
Create a list of three short reflection questions in [language] for a [grade X] class after a lesson on [specific theme, e.g. travel], to capture their thoughts.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with Social Studies teachers at a [lower secondary school] to connect [language] to [specific theme, e.g. history], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a [grade X] class's written skills in [language], with three criteria.
Write a list of five resources (e.g. apps, films) for a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class that support [specific goal, e.g. listening comprehension].
Suggest three ways to adapt a [language] lesson for a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. concentration difficulties], with one solution for each.
Create a weekly planning template for [language] for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. speaking, reading, writing).
Give me five ideas for using minimal resources in a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class to practise [specific goal, e.g. dialogues], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific language aspect, e.g. everyday phrases in [language]], with one activity for each.
Write a short guide for managing a quiet [grade X] class during a [language] lesson, with three steps and a conversation starter in the target language.
Give me an example of a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class that combines language with [specific theme, e.g. the environment], with three components.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with music teachers at a [primary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. pronunciation in [language]], with one example for each.
Create a lesson for a [grade X] class in which they write a short text in [language] about [specific theme, e.g. a day at school], with three steps and one learning goal.
Give me five ways to make a listening comprehension activity more engaging for a [grade X] class in [language], with one creative idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class to hold a simple conversation in [language] about [specific topic, e.g. free time], with three steps.
Suggest three ways to connect a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class to [specific theme, e.g. food traditions], with one activity for each.
Create a list of five short warm-up activities in [language] for a [grade X] class that reinforce [specific goal, e.g. vocabulary], with a description for each.
Give me an example of a routine for closing a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class with a short role-play dialogue, with three steps.
Suggest three ways to use digital tools (e.g. [specific tool, e.g. Kahoot]) in a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class, with one task for each.
Write a short template for a 5-minute reflection in [language] after a lesson for a [grade X] class, with three questions about their learning.
Give me five ideas for integrating games into a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class, focusing on [specific goal, e.g. grammar], with one game for each.
Create a list of three short ways to support a [grade X] student who is struggling with [specific skill, e.g. pronunciation in [language]], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with art teachers at a [primary school] to connect [language] to [specific theme, e.g. art], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple plan for teaching a [grade X] class to write a postcard in [language] over one lesson, with three steps.
Write a list of five simple texts or songs in [language] for a [grade X] class that reinforce [specific goal, e.g. listening], with a rationale for each.
Suggest three ways to handle a [grade] class that is reluctant to speak [language] during a lesson, with one method for each.
Create a term planning template for [language] for a [grade] class, with three focus areas (e.g. listening, speaking, writing) and timeframes.
Give me five ideas for creating a simple group discussion in [language] with a [grade X] class, with minimal preparation, and one topic for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific language concept, e.g. polite phrases in [language]], with one activity for each.
Write a short guide for supporting a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. language anxiety] in a [language] lesson, with three steps.
Give me an example of a [language] lesson for a [grade X] class that combines language with [specific theme, e.g. technology], with three components.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the student council at a [primary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. use of [language]], with one example for each.
